channel.title: SEAtoday - Learning & Education
channel.description: Upcoming events for SEAtoday - Learning & Education

item.title: Watts Basketball
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Step up your game Learn from Husky legend and former professional basketball player Donald Watts. Players of all skill levels are welcome to come learn the basics or fine-tune their skills. Small group and semi-private training sessions are also available. Drop-in classes held weekly on Saturdays. 9–10 am \(elementary school students\) 10–11 am \(middle school students\) 11 am–12 pm \(high school students\) Contact Athletic Programs Manager Darin Barr at dbarr@wac.net to inquire.

Event details

item.title: Women's Conference
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Join us for a day centered on Jesus, our wonderful counselor. We’ll hear from Kacey Batterton, a licensed counselor, sharing how the care Jesus modeled in scripture is echoed in how counselors are taught to care for others. Together, we’ll explore what Christlike empathy, listening, and compassion can look like for all of us. Not as experts, but as women learning to love well. This is a free, one-day event open to women of all ages \(teens 13\+ welcome\). Bring a friend, everyone is invited! Lunch isn’t included, but we’ll have a room reserved at a local restaurant for anyone who’d like to gather during the break. Come as you are. Be encouraged, equipped, and reminded of the gentle care of Jesus.

Event details

item.title: Nourishing Networks Spring 2026 NetWorkshop
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Together Center, 16307 NE 83rd St, Redmond, WA

Nourishers gather together! We're all needed now more than ever.

Event details

item.title: The Conflict Without Collapse™ Experience
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Metropolitan Park West Tower, 1761 Minor Avenue, Seattle, WA

A one-day immersive journey to transform conflict into connection through clarity, courage, and restoration.

Event details

item.title: Photography Basics for Nikon Users - LIVE
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Kenmore Camera, 6708 NE 181st St, Kenmore, WA

Class for new users of Nikon interchangeable lens cameras.

Event details

item.title: Best Day Ever: South Park
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: South Park Hall, 1253 S Cloverdale St., Seattle, WA

Join us for our first Best Day Ever event with Cultivate South Park! This exciting one-day event will feature activities like West African dance & storytelling, chocolate tasting, Duwamish River tours, local food tasting, arts & crafts, and more. Sign up now for a full day of joy and community! Best Day Ever is a one-day, community-powered experience that celebrates the vibrancy, culture, and pride of Seattle’s neighborhoods. Co-created by the Department of Neighborhoods \(DON\) and the Seattle Chamber of Connection \(SCoC\), Best Day Ever strengthens civic belonging by bringing people, place, and purpose together. Guided by DON’s Community Engagement Framework and the Chamber’s Six Points of Connection, this initiative uplifts community leadership and honors the histories, creativity, and cultural assets that make each neighborhood unique. A thriving neighborhood is one where residents feel connected, represented, and proud of where they live — and Best Day Ever is designed to bring that feeling to life. Each activation builds belonging through service, storytelling, movement, shared meals, joyful connection, and economic visibility for local small businesses.

Event details

item.title: Introduction to Pruning with Plant Amnesty
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Speaker: Kristin Jackson, Executive Director of Plant Amnesty. Presentation: Pruning makes sense when you understand the science behind it. You will be introduced to the basics of tree biology and structure, tree responses to the basic types of pruning cuts, pruning standards and, best management practice. Cost: $9.00 Register

Event details

item.title: Home buyer class up to 150k in assistance
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Join our in-person Home Buyer Class and score up to $150k in home assistance—don’t miss out!

Event details

Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Meditation, Breathwork, Cold Plunge & Community! Guided session w/ Certified Yoga, QiGong,Trauma Sensitive, Meditation & Breathwork Teacher

Monday, March 23, 2026 at 11 AM

A free informational webinar intended for building owners, managers, and industry professionals interested in learning about WA Clean Buildings Law, Energy Benchmarking, Energy Management Plans, Operatio ns & Maintenance Programs, and more. https://www.redmond.gov/calendar.aspx?EID=3438

Location: NW Rockhounds, 2720 NE 115th Suite A, Seattle, WA

Find your own rock at Carkeek Beach & turn your rough stone into a shaped and polished cabochon! This is a 2 day class \(Read details below\)

Monday, March 23, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: NW Rockhounds, 2720 NE 115th Suite A, Seattle, WA

item.title: Chai Blending Workshop
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Friday Afternoon Tea, 4228 Stone Way North, Seattle, WA

Learn about the diverse and rich world of chai and the special bond a family chai recipe forges. Blend and take home your own house chai!

Event details

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

Event details

item.title: IMPROV 100 MONDAY - Intro to Improv: Say Yes!, SPRING'26
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Improv School 5700 6th Ave S Suite 109 Seattle 98108, 5700 6th Ave S Suite 109, Seattle, WA

Say yes! Learn to listen, connect, play and use your own life to create stories with others! An 8 week intro to improvised theatre!

item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 9 AM

Attendees will participate in a 4-day conference that will focus on HOC curriculum adaptation, implementation, and facilitation.

item.title: Hyper-Local Happy Hour (Non-alcoholic)
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Calvary: The Hill, 1107 E Howell St, Seattle, WA

Get to know your hyper-local Capitol Hill neighbors around Cal Anderson Park in an open house style, non-alcoholic happy hour!  Enjoy non-alcoholic beers and yummy mocktails, along with a charcuterie spread, all inside a 120-year-old historic Capitol Hill church building. These happy hours will be from 4-5:30pm every 4th Tuesday through June  January 27th February 24th March 24th April 28th May 26th June 23rd \*Please note that ADA access is available into the happy hour area, but not to the restrooms unfortunately \(very old building!\)

Event details

item.title: Kirkland Business Pitch Competition 2026
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Kirkland Performance Center, 350 Kirkland Ave, Kirkland, WA

Join us for a transformative Pitch Competition at the Kirkland Performance Center, designed for business owners, property owners, community members, investors, and all who are interested in helping a business grow! Tickets: Registration attend as an audience member is requested but not required. Date: Pitch Competition: March 24, 5:30 PM Doors Open at Kirkland Performance Center Pitch Categories: People’s Choice Start-Up Expansion Prizes: Top finalists in each category win prizes of $5,000! Networking Opportunities: Connect with fellow entrepreneurs, industry experts, local mentors, and potential collaborators. Let’s grow, innovate, and collaborate! For more information, contact Christian Lash at Clash@kirklandwa.gov

Event details

item.title: Tech Tuesdays at a/stir on Capitol Hill
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Capitol Cider, 818 E Pike St, Seattle, WA

Craving real face time after too many Zooms? Come hang out at a/stir on Broadway for an easygoing tech-community mixer.

Event details

item.title: ONLINE: Winter Creative Workspace 2026 - A 12
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Fremont Abbey Arts Center, 4272 Fremont Ave North, Seattle, WA

Event details

item.title: IMPROV 100 TUESDAY - Intro to Improv: Say Yes!, SPRING'26
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley, Seattle, WA

Say yes! Learn to listen, connect, play and use your own life to create stories with others! An 8 week intro to improvised theatre! Discover Unexpected Productions' philosophy of improvisation, developed over our 43 years of improvising in Seattle! You'll learn how to actively listen, to use your own life to create collaborative stories, and you'll work on the fundamental principles of improvisation: what it means to say, "Yes, And", how to tell stories that include everyone in the room, how to stop worrying about failing, and the games and concepts behind what we do on stages around the world! Under the guidance of one of our best Seattle improv teachers, you will learn to:· -Be a Better Listener -Conquer Fears -Spark Creativity -Meet New People & Make New Friends -Become a Better Problem Solver -Gain Confidence· -Fail With Grace \(and Maybe Even Enjoy It\) -Say Yes More in Your Life -Get Out of Your Comfort Zone -Become a Better Public Speaker -Discover More About Yourself -...and have a blast doing it! Time and Dates : Tuesdays for eight \(8\) weeks, from March 24 to May 22, 7pm to 9:30pm NOTE: Attendance at the first session of your course is mandatory as it sets the agenda for the entire quarter. If you think you will have to miss the first class, please consider registering for the next quarter. No Prerequisites. All students begin at this level, even those with existing improv experience. It’s a prerequisite to the other levels. Location: 5700 6th Ave St, Suite 109, Umbrella Room, Seattle, WA 98108 \(In Georgetown, south of downtown, with plenty of parking.\) Maximum Students: 16 Cost: Ticket price $295.00 Age: 18 and above \*\*Cancellation policy: If you must cancel before the first class, we can only offer credit towards the same level class next quarter. We are unable to offer refunds. If you cancel after the first class we cannot offer credit towards a future class. Again, we are unable to offer refunds.

Event details

item.title: IMPROV 400: Stories that Matter, Tuesday, Spring '26
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 7 PM

Stories that Matter: Learn the skills and techniques to create collaborative longform, personal monologues, and more theatrical performances

item.title: 2026 March Chamber Mixer and Small Business Resource Fair
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 3:30 PM

Location: DoubleTree Hotel, SeaTac, 18740 International Blvd, Seattle, WA

Chamber Mixer - March \+ Small Business Resource Fair Join us for our March Chamber Mixer bringing together local professionals, entrepreneurs, and community leaders for an evening of connection, collaboration, and valuable business resources. This month's mixer features a Small Business Resource Fair, where attendees can meet representatives from organizations offering tools, programs, and guidance to help small businesses grow and thrive. Whether you're looking to expand your network, explore partnership opportunities, or learn about business support services, this event provides a welcoming environment to make meaningful connections. Enjoy light refreshments, engage with fellow professionals, and discover new resources to support your business journey-all while taking in insights from the Port of Seattle about upcoming initiatives that impact our regional economy. Perfect for: ✔ Small business owners ✔ Startups and entrepreneurs ✔ Professionals looking to network ✔ Anyone interested in local business resources We look forward to seeing you there! BUSINESS INVOLVEMENT OPPORTUNITIES: Sponsorship opportunities are still available, for more information please download our Seattle Southside Chamber Event Sponsorship Form.

Event details

item.title: 2026 March Chamber Mixer Port of Seattle and Small Business Resource Fair
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 3:30 PM

Location: DoubleTree Hotel, SeaTac, WA

Chamber Mixer - March \(Port of Seattle\) \+ Small Business Resource Fair Join us for our March Chamber Mixer hosted in partnership with the Port of Seattle, bringing together local professionals, entrepreneurs, and community leaders for an evening of connection, collaboration, and valuable business resources. This month's mixer features a Small Business Resource Fair, where attendees can meet representatives from organizations offering tools, programs, and guidance to help small businesses grow and thrive. Whether you're looking to expand your network, explore partnership opportunities, or learn about business support services, this event provides a welcoming environment to make meaningful connections. Enjoy light refreshments, engage with fellow professionals, and discover new resources to support your business journey-all while taking in insights from the Port of Seattle about upcoming initiatives that impact our regional economy. Perfect for: ✔ Small business owners ✔ Startups and entrepreneurs ✔ Professionals looking to network ✔ Anyone interested in local business resources and Port of Seattle programs We look forward to seeing you there! BUSINESS INVOLVEMENT OPPORTUNITIES: Sponsorship opportunities are still available, for more information please download our Seattle Southside Chamber Event Sponsorship Form.

Event details

item.title: Understanding the Custom Home Process
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Learn how to build your dream home at our in-person Custom Homes 101 seminar, hosted inside a JayMarc model home. Free to attend!

Event details

item.title: Business After Hours: Virginia Mason Franciscan Health
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Description Feeling healthy? Let&'s find out at March&'s Business After Hours hosted by Virginia Mason Franciscan Health at their West Seattle location, where clinical care meets community focused service. VMFH is known for delivering high quality, patient centered healthcare while investing deeply in prevention, access, and partnerships that strengthen the neighborhoods they serve, including West Seattle. In person networking remains effective because face to face interaction improves trust, clarity, and follow up, a conclusion supported by Harvard Business Review research comparing in person and virtual business relationships. This evening offers space for thoughtful, healthy conversation and relationship building!

Event details

item.title: Business After Hours: VMFH
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Description Networking is a vital component of career success, with 80% of professionals considering it essential and nearly 100% believing that face-to-face meetings build stronger long-term relationships. Let&'s start 2026 out strong with some new connections!

Event details

item.title: HAPPY HOUR
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Description FREE Event for Lynnwood Chamber Members & their Guests - please register in advance.

Event details

item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Seattle Design Center, 5701 6th Ave S, Seattle, WA

Showroom events, AI keynote with Jenna Gaidusek, and the live announcement of the 2025 Northwest Design Awards winners.

item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Suquamish Clearwater Casino Resort, 15347 Suquamish Way Northeast, Suquamish, WA

Attendees will participate in a 4-day conference that will focus on HOC curriculum adaptation, implementation, and facilitation.

Event details

item.title: Living Large with Less Stuff
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Downsize to the right size! Take the stress out of de-cluttering with easy steps and practical advice for a successful transition. During this event, you will hear from the following speakers: Catherine Arendt, downsizing expert and Aljoya’s At Your Service Manager, works with families from start to finish as they transition from their homes to retirement communities. Nicole Bates, Relocation Consultant at Hansen Bros. Moving & Storage and HB Move Management, has assisted thousands of clients with their moves and specializes in working with clients downsizing from a long-time home to a smaller living space. Complimentary valet parking will be provided for your convenience.

Event details

item.title: Family Business Exchange | Webinar
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 12 PM

Save the Date! About the Family Business Exchange Seattle University’s Family Business Exchange \(FBX\) is a community of Pacific Northwest family business owners who gather to engage in education, networking, and professional development tailored specifically to their needs. FBX provides opportunities for families to address their unique challenges through relevant programming. It serves as a forum for information exchange with the goal of helping each member thrive through future generations. Areas of best practice that we cover include family business succession planning, governance, leadership development and growth, among others. About Assured Partners Assured Partners is a Seattle-based insurance brokerage that provides tailored commercial insurance, employee benefits, risk management and personal insurance solutions to businesses and individuals, drawing on the expertise of a national partnership of independent brokers. Please contact Nick Brewe to connect with Assured Partners about your business insurance needs. This event is open to FBX members and prospective members.

Event details

item.title: Electric SEA
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 12:01 PM

Please join us for Electric SEA 2026, an arts-oriented hackathon—part creative retreat, part pop-up lab, part public art salon.

Event details

item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Fidélitas, 14200 Northeast 145th Street #Suite B, Woodinville, WA

Come create a blooming beautiful design - celebrate Spring with our floral class

Event details

item.title: Transform Your Greenery: A Creative Copper Plant Stick Workshop
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by Amanda Long of Amanda Metals. Join us for an engaging and creative experience in this copper plant stick workshop! You will be creating three different copper and crystal glass bead plant sticks that will transform your greenery into stunning displays. Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced creator, this workshop is designed to inspire your creativity. Let’s unleash your creative side and bring a touch of whimsy to your plants! All materials provided. $91 BBGS members/$130 nonmembers.

Event details

item.title: Hot Open Studio
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: NW Rockhounds, 2720 NE 115th Suite A, Seattle, WA

Designed for silversmithing students who want to practice & pursue independent projects \( Level 1-Meet the Butane Torch class Required\)

Event details

item.title: Electric SEA
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 12:01 PM

Please join us for Electric SEA 2026, an arts-oriented hackathon—part creative retreat, part pop-up lab, part public art salon.

Event details

item.title: Pilates for Gardeners
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by Jo Anne Iaciofano. Grab your mat, kick off your shoes, and join this Pilates-inspired class to improve body movement, core strength, and muscle control. This beginner’s friendly class, using mind-body connection and breath, will take you through a series of exercises with an emphasis on technique and fluidity. We will focus on those areas most stressed during gardening by using your core to power the muscles increasing your energy and endurance. All levels are welcome. Wear comfortable clothing and be prepared to workout barefoot. Please bring a yoga or Pilates mat, towel, and water. This class will take place in the Aaron Education Center.  $17.50 BBGS Member/$25 Non-Member.

Event details

item.title: BiggerPockets Value-Add Conference
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Seattle, Wa, 98121, Seattle, WA

Real estate investors looking to sharpen their execution and scale for smarter deals will not want to miss the **first-ever BiggerPockets Value-Add Conference** in Seattle on March 28, 2026. This one-day, tight-knit event has only 120 seats available and is designed specifically for experienced investors actively executing deals. Led by seasoned local investor and Million Dollar Zombie Flips host James Dainard, alongside BiggerPockets experts Dave Meyer and Henry Washington, the conference offers a rare opportunity to learn how top investors scale deals in today’s competitive real estate market through stronger underwriting, strategic renovations, optimized financing, and disciplined execution systems. Investment to attend the one-day conference starts at $500, with a limited number of spots available HERE.  The in-person event features powerful speakers, delivering  practical, action-based strategies investors can implement immediately, including: Team building and legal structure for sustainable growth Underwriting and deal structuring across asset types Capital stack and financing strategies (hard money, DSCR, BRRRR) Construction and project management systems that protect margins Renovations and design decisions that directly impact ROI Scaling operations with stronger vendor management and streamlined processes This experience is designed for investors with three or more completed deals who are actively executing BRRRRs, flips, or small-scale development projects and looking to build repeatable, scalable systems. With Seattle’s competitive housing market and continued investor activity, the conference offers timely insight into how local operators are navigating tighter margins, rising costs, and evolving financing strategies. In addition to the conference, on March 29, a limited Premium Property Tour will take attendees inside active Seattle investment properties, where James and the speakers will walk through real deals and answer questions in small groups.

Event details

item.title: COUTURE MASTER SEWING CLASSES: (BEGINNER: Standard & Intensive)
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: 2265 116th Ave NE, 2265 116th Avenue Northeast, Bellevue, WA

Hands-on couture sewing class covering garment construction, fit, and finishing for beginners and creatives.

Event details

item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Hidden Valley Office Center, 1750 112th Avenue Northeast, Bellevue, WA

Join us for a workshop of connection and intimacy at Hold Me Tight, where we'll co-create a safe space to embrace vulnerability and strength

item.title: Master Your Camera Controls and Composition in the Field - Photo Workshops
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: BLANK, 2001 Western Avenue, Seattle, WA

Photography workshops lead by a professional photographer. Instructional sessions on camera controls followed by hands-on practice sessions.

Event details

item.title: Connect for Good: Sammamish Nonprofit Expo
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: SPC/Sammamish Presbyterian Church, 22522 NE Inglewood Hill Rd, Sammamish, WA

Connect for Good: Sammamish Nonprofit Expo 2026 Sponsored by: Puget Sound Energy, Sammamish Friends and Lead-ology Mark your calendar and join us for Connect for Good, a community-wide nonprofit expo designed to bring people, purpose, and impact together. On Saturday, March 28, from 10:00am–2:00pm, the Sammamish community is invited to gather at Sammamish Presbyterian Church for an inspiring afternoon of connection and service. Explore 30\+ local nonprofits, discover meaningful volunteer opportunities, and learn how you—and your family—can make a difference close to home. The event will feature youth leadership and student involvement opportunities, along with a Youth Zone & activities, making it welcoming and engaging for all ages. Whether you’re a student, family, community member, or volunteer, Connect for Good is the perfect place to support causes you care about, get involved in service, and strengthen our local community—all in one place. Hosted by the Sammamish Chamber of Commerce, in collaboration with local nonprofits and community partners.Nonprofit participation and sponsorship opportunities available.

Event details

item.title: Peninsula Birth, Baby, & Beyond Expo
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Kitsap Sun Pavilion, 1200 Fairgrounds Rd NW, Bremerton, WA

Come check out the Peninsula Birth, Baby & Beyond Expo for all things related to pregnancy, birth, and parenting in one convenient location!

Event details

item.title: Homebuyer Education Workshop [VIRTUAL]
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

This is a free five-hour workshop to help you meet the WA State Housing Finance Commission’s education requirements for down payment assistance and a variety of affordable housing programs and opportunities for qualified applicants! These requirements include the successful completion of a Homebuyers Education Seminar meeting the current FHA, Fannie Mae, FreddieMac, and SMBA benchmark standards.  At the conclusion of the workshop, participants will receive a certificate of achievement from the WA State Housing Finance Commission and DFI valid for two years from the date of issuance.  To join our next workshop, please complete and submit the following registration form at least 24-hours before your selected workshop date. Once this form has been submitted, be sure to check your email for a digital confirmation, as well as additional follow up and important information from a ULMS Housing Department staff member.   Virtual workshops are held every 3rd and 4th Saturday of the month from 10:00AM – 3:30PM. A Zoom link will be sent to you via a ULMS Housing department staff member upon completing registration.

Event details

item.title: Hold Me Tight
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Hidden Valley Office Center, 1750 112th Avenue Northeast, Bellevue, WA

Join us for a workshop of connection and intimacy at Hold Me Tight, where we'll co-create a safe space to embrace vulnerability and strength

Event details

item.title: Issaquah Highlands LEGO® Expo
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Blakely Hall At Issaquah Highlands, 2550 NE Park Dr, Issaquah, WA

Calling all LEGO® hobbyists, fans and families! All ages welcome to the biggest LEGO event ever held in Issaquah! The fun includes hands-on activities with a giant free-build area, LEGO displays and models, shopping and photo ops. The event is free to attend. Attendees will be admitted as space allows. Do you have a LEGO build worth sharing? We're now accepting exhibitor applications. Apply here: https://issaquahhighlands.com/?p=65836 Thanks to our sponsors for making this event possible: Highlands Fiber Network, Issaquah Ice Cream Trike and Bricks and Minifigs.

Event details

item.title: Natasha Zimmers with Naja Lund Aparico - 'Tiny Hiker' and 'Seasons by the Lake: Adventures in Greenl
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Third Place Books Lake Forest Park, 17171 Bothell Way NE,, Lake Forest Park, WA

Welcome spring by celebrating the joy of exploring the great outdoors! Third Place Books welcomes authors Natasha Zimmers and Naja Lund Aparico to our Lake Forest Park store for a storytime featuring Natasha's book Tiny Hikers and Naja's book Seasons by the Lake: Adventures in Greenland. This event is free and open to the public. RSVP is not required. Readers of all ages welcome! About Tiny Hikers . . . "Offers a realistic, joyful depiction of a young adventurer and reflects the author's experience as an outdoor educator. A standout debut that will inspire young readers to explore the outdoors." -- School Library Journal \(SLJ\), starred review The tiny hiker finishes loading up her pack and excitedly gets on the trail, leading her family through the ancient trees toward their beach campsite. At first it's fun to explore--she finds a hollow log, wriggles under a fallen tree, and spots a slimy slug But then the trail starts getting steeper, and when her legs get tired, she trips on a root and takes a hard fall. Now her knee hurts, her elbows hurt...everything hurts She just wants to give up. But after a little break, a tasty snack and an exciting glimpse of the beach, the tiny hiker feels better and decides she will make it. With one final scamper over some giant beach logs, she finds the beach. Time to play And at the end of the long day, after sitting by the campfire, the tiny hiker gets cozy in her sleeping bag and falls fast asleep. About Seasons by the Lake. . . Spend a year experiencing the seasons in Greenland with two Inuit siblings. Brothers Minik and Nuka have a whole year of adventures ahead in Greenland, including finding ice shards from glaciers in the spring to picking crowberries during the summer when the sun barely sets. The darkness of winter reveals Arsarnerit, or the Aurora Borealis, above as the two whisper about the Inuit myth behind the lights. Greenlandic Inuk author Naja Lund Aparico combines a sweet sibling story with an exploration of the seasons in the Arctic featuring stunning illustrations by German and Choctaw illustrator Alex Nees that captures the sweeping landscape. Natasha Zimmers loves being outside in all seasons and weather, but her favourite place to be is on a wild west coast beach, backpacking with her own not-so-tiny hikers. She teaches outdoor education and loves seeing the world through the eyes of her students as they discover the wonders of nature. Naja Lund Aparico is a Greenlandic Inuk author with a background in international relations and currently a second year MFA graduate student at the Institute of American Indian Arts \(IAIA\) with a focus on creative non-fiction. Having grown up in Greenland, she now lives in the Pacific Northwest with her family. Aparico’s children’s books and essays are centered on the indigenous identity with an emphasis on kin, heritage, and the environment. Her US debut picture book, SEASONS BY THE LAKE, will be published by Dial / PRH in March of 2026, while her second picture book \(untitled\) is set to be published by Candlewick in late Spring of 2027, and her third picture book will be published by Dial/PRH in late 2027. Want a signed edition of the featured book, but can't make it to the event? Order through our website or over the phone, and write your request for a signature or personalization in the comments field at checkout. Please call the hosting store if you're placing your order within 24 hours of the event. For media or access inquiries, please email events@thirdplacebooks.com or call our Lake Forest Park store at \(206\) 366-3311.

Event details

item.title: WSMA Math Fair 2026
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Eastlake High School, 400 228th Avenue Northeast, Sammamish, WA

MATH FAIR 2026 \(Sharing for Elementary & Middle School Families\) Hosted at Eastlake High School, Sammamish, WA Join the community for an exciting Math Fair happening on: March 29, 2026 ⏰ 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM 400 228th Ave NE, Sammamish, WA 98074 This annual event \(organized by Washington State Math Association – WSMA\) brings students together to showcase math projects in a fun, science-fair–style format. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ THEME: Structures & Systems Students explore how mathematical structures power real-world applications — engineering, architecture, computer systems, and more! ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ COMPETITION DIVISIONS • Grades 3–5 • Grades 6–8 Teams of 1–4 students Projects can include trifolds, posters, models, or demonstrations Awards will be given in each division. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ HOW TO PARTICIPATE ✔ Submit a team project \(Grades 3–8\) ✔ Attend and explore projects, interactive math games & activities ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ⏳ IMPORTANT DEADLINES Registration: March 15, 2026 Project Proposal: March 22, 2026 Sharing for families who may be interested — this looks like a wonderful opportunity for kids who enjoy building, designing, explaining ideas, and presenting math creatively!

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

item.title: Matchmaking Your Business Build your network. Save time. Strengthen your resources.
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Mercer Island Community Center, 2247 84th Ave SE, Mercer Island, WA

Matchmaking Your Business is a structured, high-energy networking event designed to connect Realtors with trusted industry vendors. Location: Mercer Island Community Center 8236 SE 24th Street, Mercer Island, WA 98040 Parking: Free When: Tuesday, March 31st, 2026﻿ ﻿Time: 11am to 1pm Matchmaking Your Business is a structured, high-energy networking event designed to connect Realtors with trusted industry vendors. The goal is simple: Help Realtors build reliable vendor partnerships that elevate the client experience, streamline transactions, and support long-term business growth. Tired of calling vendor after vendor trying to find someone reliable for your clients? Come meet them in one place. Our WCR Seattle Metro Vendor Speed Networking Event gives you the chance to connect one-on-one with trusted industry professionals who can help support your business and your clients. Build your network. Save time. Strengthen your resources. Just real connections that build your book of business. Come ready to connect, collaborate, and fine the right matches for your business.

Event details

item.title: Power Connect: Bombo
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 4:30 PM

Location: Bombo Italian Kitchen, 1600 9th Ave, Seattle, WA

Description Power Connect at Bombo Italian Date: Tuesday, March 31, 2026 Time: 4:30 PM Location: Bombo Italian Presented by: U.S. Bank Tickets: $25 for members and $35 for guests Join GSBA for a new Power Connect at Bombo Italian on Tuesday, March 31 at 4:30 PM. Power Connect is our signature networking series-an easygoing space to meet fellow LGBTQ\+ and allied professionals, reconnect with familiar faces, and build relationships that support your business and our community. Come as you are, bring a colleague, and expect the standard Power Connect vibes: warm welcomes, real conversation, and connections that keep going after the event. What to Expect A welcoming, relationship-first environment Space for casual conversation and meaningful introductions A great chance to meet GSBA members, partners, and friends An easy way to plug into the GSBA community Accessibility If you have access needs or accommodation requests, please reach out to GSBA in advance and we'll do our best to support you: membership@thegsb.org Thank You to Our Presenting Sponsor Power Connect: Bombo is presented by U.S. Bank, supporting LGBTQ\+ and allied businesses and helping strengthen connections across our region.

Event details

item.title: Maddox Batson
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Crystal Ballroom, Seattle, WA

Maddox Batson, a teen sensation born in Nashville and raised in Birmingham, Alabama, combines modern neo-traditional Country music with a touch of southern rock and pop. He's a versatile talent, excelling as a singer/songwriter, multi-instrument musician, actor and athlete. Batson's humor and charm shine through his viral social media videos, amassing over 200M views in under five months and over 3M followers. Born to entertain, he brings a unique style that sets him apart. Touted as a "mix between Cole Swindell, Morgan Wallen and Justin Bieber," he is continuing to hone his craft, both as a vocalist and as a songwriter for his ever-growing fan base and hopes to win you over with his genuine smile and infectious voice. In addition to his two viral track releases "Tears In The River" and "I Wanna Know," Batson has also solidified his spot in the writers room, most recently landing a cut from global superstars Lana Del Ray and Quavo with "Tough." The rising star will join Gabby Barrett on stage this August with more dates to be announced.

item.title: Artist's Way Classes Winter 2026 are Now Open for Registration
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: The Fauntleroy Schoolhouse, 9131 California Avenue SW, Seattle, WA

The Artist's Way Classes for Winter 2026 via Zoom or In-Person are Now Open for Registration The Artist's Way by Julia Cameron is an international bestseller on the subject of creativity. Whether you see yourself as an artist or not, this book and class can be an incredibly useful resource to tap into your creative side which can benefit many parts of your life. Led by Creativity Instructor, Kate Gavigan, the 2 hour a week 12 week long class will take students through the 12 chapters with an emphasis on the accompanying chapter exercises and additional creativity exercises and strategies \(i.e. meditation tools\). This material can work with anyone, no matter how dormant or energized their creative life may be.  Students report that as a result of going through our Artist's Way class they more fully and genuinely know themselves, they discover or re-discover their gifts and talents and have more fun and joy in their lives. To quote Julia, "In order to retrieve your creativity, you need to find it." The basic tools and the additional creativity exercises that we incorporate into the 12 week course can help you do just that. No prerequisites required for this class.  Students also meet with the instructor for a one on one chat on Zoom for 30 minutes prior to the first class and before the last class. Section I: January 19 – April 6 – via Zoom Monday Mornings, 10:00 am – noon $395 \(12 weeks\) OR Section II:  January 19 – April 6 - via Zoom Monday Evenings, 6:30 – 8:30 pm \(Pacific Standard time\) $395 \(12 weeks\) OR Section III:  – January 21 – April 8 via Zoom Wednesdays, 3:00 – 5:00 pm \(Pacific Standard time\) $395 \(12 weeks\) OR Section IV: January 21 – April 8 via In-PERSON Wednesdays, 6:30 – 8:30 pm \(Pacific Standard time\) $395 \(12 weeks\) This In-Person Class held in West Seattle at the Fauntleroy Schoolhouse, 9131 California Ave. SW, Seattle, WA Instructor for all sections: Kate Gavigan Note: Students will use the book The Artist’s Way by Julia Cameron. Students are responsible for purchasing their own copy of The Artist’s Way by Julia Cameron. To Register or for more information: https://seattleartistswaycenter.com/class-offerings For testimonials from past students: https://seattleartistswaycenter.com/class-testimonials Kate Gavigan is passionate about The Artist's Way after offering classes since 2009 and seeing the positive changes in her students' lives and having personally experienced the benefits of the materials. Kate worked in social services as a trainer for over 10 years when she found herself drawn to the material in The Artist's Way, which helped her uncover a passion for theatre and the arts. She credits her mid-life career change \(now working at her dream jobs as an Artist's Way Instructor and Speaker since 2009, a Creativity Coach, a Home Organizer and pursuing her interest in Acting and Music\) to having gone through The Artist's Way.

item.title: Norwegian Language Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

This nine-week series will allow you to jump into the basics of Norwegian language and culture in a supportive, face-to-face classroom environment. Students will learn essential vocabulary, everyday phrases, pronunciation, and simple grammar structures through interactive activities, conversation practice, and cultural activities. No prior foreign language experience is required; ages 16+. Every Thursday, April 2 - May 28 NOR1A: 5-6:30pm - NOR1A is for absolute beginners with no prior language experience NOR1C: 6:45-8pm - NOR1C is for students who may know some basic Norwegian and want to grow their skills

Event details

item.title: Spacesuit Design Contest Workshop
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Museum of Flight, 9404 E Marginal Way S, Seattle, WA

Need help or inspiration with your design for the Spacesuit Design Contest? Join us for our NEW Free First Thursday drop-in workshops! Museum staff will lead workshops to help contestants learn about different important parts of a spacesuit, ask questions, think through ideas and start to figure how to build their own spacesuits. About Spacesuit Design Contest For this contest, entrants must design and draw an original Spacesuit that follows the theme and all Contest requirements and rules. The top entrants in each category will then build their design as a wearable, but not functional, spacesuit. The Top 5 Spacesuit designs in each category will be showcased in a Fashion Show on Saturday, June 27, 2026. A panel of judges will decide the winning design for each age category \(four categories total\) and the four winning designs will be showcased at Fashion in Flight in November 2026.

Event details

item.title: LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 7 AM

Location: Lake Washington Institute of Technology - LWTech, 11605 132nd Ave NE, Kirkland, WA

LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP Friday, April 3, 2026 Lake Washington Institute of Technology – 11605 132nd Avenue NE, Kirkland, WA 98034-8506 TITLE SPONSOR - DURANTA The Washington Association of Landscape Professionals is excited to offer the LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP training in partnership with the National Association of Landscape Professionals. WALP will be offering the LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP in APRIL 3, 2026 at Lake Washington Insitute of Technology that will be comprehensive training for your staff and workforce. Hands-On Training For Your Crews! The new Landscape Technician Boot Camp will provide hands-on training for your crews. Based on the curriculum for the Landscape Industry Certified Technician Exterior exam, this industry-vetted training will help prepare your crews to work more effectively in 2026. Boot Camp includes training in techniques and safety in 15 different competency areas organized into 3 disciplines presented in Spanish and English! Bonus! This course also helps prepare people for the Landscape Industry Certified Technician Exterior exam. So, once you’ve trained your team, consider registering them to become certified through the National Association of Landscape Professionals \(NALP\). Registration Costs: WALP Members \(First 5 registrants\): $95.00 per person WALP Members \(6\+ registrants\): $80.00 per person NON-WALP Members : $145.00 per person

item.title: Microwave
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Showbox SoDo, 1700 1st Ave. South, Seattle, WA

If music can serve as a release for its creator and listener alike, then Microwave's new album is sonic catharsis incarnate. Death Is A Warm Blanket is an explosion of mental and physical frustration, channelled into ten dynamic tracks that show just how powerful, gut-wrenching--and fun--loud rock music can be when it's this completely unbridled. Vocalist/guitarist Nathan Hardy, bassist Tyler Hill, and drummer Timothy Pittard formed Microwave in Atlanta, Georgia in 2012 as an extension of friendships, school, and the local music scene. The band's earlier work \(including 2014's Stovall and 2016's Much Love\) naturally reflected a more youthful outlook--romances, the many anxieties of your early 20s--but also the fallout that followed Hardy's exit from the Mormon church at age 22. As Microwave began to pick up steam, Hardy underwent a sharp change in worldview fueled by the upheaval of entering adulthood, an amplified desire for new experiences after a particularly protected upbringing, and the easy access to drugs and alcohol on the road. "You're hopeful when you're younger and it made our earlier stuff a little more light at the end of the tunnel," Hardy explains. "When I left the church, it was exciting to experience all these new things but then six years go by, that wears off, you're older and you don't have health insurance, and you realize everything didn't get better." While Microwave continued to grow and tour steadily with each release, Hardy's body and frame of mind deteriorated. His shoulder dislocated during the band's performance at the Wrecking Ball festival in Atlanta and the absence of any kind of financial safety net exacerbated his lengthy recovery from surgery. "Being in a band isn't a lifestyle that leads to comforts," Hardy says, "I was sleeping on couches. I'm a stagehand so if I can't lift anything, I can't work." Microwave began touring again, and the physical toll of being on the road furthered his sense of decline. "I was so unhealthy...my shoulder was still weak, I was drinking way too much, my immune system was a mess. We were trying to get through sets but I would get heat-induced vertigo because my kidneys weren't working properly. I thought I was going to die or have to stop being in a band because of it." But like so many musicians, Hardy turned towards his only reliable outlet for exorcising frustration, and Death Is A Warm Blanket began to take shape. Galvanized by a strangely motivational union of fatalism, dissatisfaction, and determination, Hardy began writing songs and slowly, but surely improving his health. The new music outlined a different version of Microwave: angrier and heavier, but still rooted in truthful songwriting and Hardy's knack for acute hooks. "When it comes to writing music there's something liberating about feeling like you're on a sinking ship. We felt totally unencumbered by any sonic expectations and this record ended up naturally heavier because of everything that was going on." The band teamed with Matt Goldman at Glow In The Dark Studios to capture Pittard's massive drum sounds before diving into Death Is A Warm Blanket's endless array of guitar tones at the home studio of guitarist Travis Hill \(who engineered Microwave's previous albums before joining the band\). Microwave put the open- ended time in the studio to good use, experimenting with unusual production flourishes and instrumental textures as they honed their new direction. The resulting album is an amalgam of everything exhilarating about loud guitars. Microwave revel in their newfound creative abandon, colliding post-hardcore aggression with '90s alternative sneer, and bleeding broodingly atmospheric verses into anthemic choruses. The band's music has always shown hints of sharper edges, but Death Is A Warm Blanket takes those loud/quiet building blocks and twists them into a snarling mass of gigantic dynamic shifts and otherworldly heaviness. "Leather Daddy" opens the album with an unassuming acoustic guitar and Hardy's reserved vocals to serve as a shaky bridge from the band's previous work, and slowly grows taut with Hill's gnarled bass before outright collapsing under the weight of colossal guitars and cacophonous drums. All the while the song's lyrics set the scene for Death Is A Warm Blanket: mental isolation, physical incapacitation, a faded sense of community, and the unexpected freedom of having very little to lose. "Float To The Top" and "Hate TKO" explore the potentially dogmatic nature of subcultures, modern social media burnout, and the absurdities of bad faith arguing. The latter conjures up an In Utero-style blend of chaos and melody, while the former's pulsating nest of guitars have been mutated to resemble a synth so ugly, it would make Trent Reznor proud. The quasi-title track "DIAWB" and "The Brakeman Has Resigned" paint a harrowing picture of Hardy's lowest moments of limping from sickness to sickness, show to show--self-medicating and resigning himself to a hopeless existence, but somehow putting one foot in front of the other. "Pull" offers a brief respite in the guitar attack, a moody slow burner that starts with some of the album's most delicate music before hurtling into some of its most destructive. Elsewhere "Mirrors" is perhaps the finest example of Microwave's mastery of hooks and heaviness: a swaggering cut of Headbanger's Ball fodder sure to incite singalongs and pandemonium alike. Death Is A Warm Blanket reaches its apocalyptic conclusion with "Part of It," a gleefully nihilistic track with sights set on an end of the world that can't help but feel ever-closer these days. The album doesn't have a light at the end of the tunnel, yet it does seem to find some semblance of acceptance even in harsh realities. "A lot of bands I grew up with had these climactic coming of age records where the sky opens up and it all turns out ok. This record doesn't have that, but I feel like that's more honest." He adds with a bit of a laugh, "It's more like someone punching you in the stomach and knocking the air out of you." Perhaps it's that painful honesty at the center of Death Is A Warm Blanket that makes it so compelling--if the end is near, Microwave won't be going quietly.

Event details
